_doc: Lattice + space group reference data for tools/twin-law-check.mjs.
Each entry is a single crystallographic refinement: unit-cell parameters
(Å for lengths, ° for angles), space-group symbol in Hermann-Mauguin
form, and the literature citation the values came from. The tool reads
this file, computes structural twin-plane candidates from the lattice
geometry, and compares them against declared twin_laws in
Initial population (this commit): 17 minerals — the canonical cubic
spinel-law set, the aragonite-group pseudo-hex carbonates, plus a
monoclinic / tetragonal / trigonal / orthorhombic sample. Adamite is
included so the v139 {101} back-test runs automatically. Spinel,
staurolite, gypsum, orthoclase are included even though they don't
have twin_laws entries in minerals.json yet — they're known structural
reference points and the tool will skip them gracefully.
Future populations should add: the remaining 142 minerals-with-twins.
Strategy: do them in batches matching the v137-v141 batch boundaries
(sulfides, phosphates, arsenates, sulfates, others). Each lattice value
must cite a real source. See v142 citation-conservatism rule.

spinel._source: Wikipedia spinel; matches Bosi et al. 2019 (Mineralogical Magazine 83:687-704) structural refinement

magnetite._source: Fleet 1981 (Acta Cryst. B37:917-920) Fe3O4 refinement at 298 K

fluorite._source: standard CaF2 refinement; Anthony Handbook v.III fluorite entry

galena._source: Noda et al. 1987 PbS refinement; Anthony Handbook v.I galena entry

aragonite._source: De Villiers 1971 (Amer. Mineral. 56:758-767) aragonite at 25 °C; Wikipedia aragonite

cerussite._source: Antao & Hassan 2009 (Canadian Mineralogist 47:1245-1255) orthorhombic ACO3 isostructural series

witherite._source: Antao & Hassan 2009 (Canadian Mineralogist 47:1245-1255) BaCO3 entry in the same series

strontianite._source: Antao & Hassan 2009 (Canadian Mineralogist 47:1245-1255) SrCO3 entry in the same series

marcasite._source: Rieder et al. 2007 (Canadian Mineralogist 45:1395-1407) marcasite refinement

adamite._source: Hill 1976 (Amer. Mineral. 61:979-986) adamite structure; matches crystalsymmetry.wordpress.com and Anthony Handbook v.IV

cassiterite._source: Bolzan et al. 1997 (Acta Cryst. B53:373-380) rutile-type oxide refinement series

calcite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V calcite entry; hex setting

quartz._source: Le Page & Donnay 1976 (Acta Cryst. B32:2456-2459) α-quartz refinement

gypsum._source: Cole & Lancucki 1974 (Acta Cryst. B30:921-929) CaSO4·2H2O refinement

orthoclase._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.II orthoclase entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); representative high-Or feldspar values

tremolite._source: Hawthorne 1981 (Reviews in Mineralogy v.9A, amphibole crystal chemistry); Anthony Handbook v.II tremolite entry

actinolite._source: Tremolite-actinolite solid-solution series (Hawthorne 1981 + 2007); slight cell expansion vs. tremolite from Fe²⁺-for-Mg substitution; Anthony Handbook v.II actinolite entry

halite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III halite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); canonical NaCl rock-salt structure, standard reference

sylvite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III sylvite entry; KCl, rock-salt structure isotype with halite

atacamite._source: Parise & Hyde 1986 (Acta Cryst. C42:1277-1280) atacamite refinement; Anthony Handbook v.III atacamite entry

borax._source: Morimoto 1956, The Crystal Structure of Borax (Min. J. Japan 2:1-18); Anthony Handbook v.V borax entry

lepidocrocite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III lepidocrocite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); γ-FeOOH layered structure

native_copper._source: Anthony Handbook v.I native copper entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); standard FCC metal structure

native_gold._source: Anthony Handbook v.I gold entry; standard FCC metal structure

native_silver._source: Anthony Handbook v.I silver entry; standard FCC metal structure

native_bismuth._source: Anthony Handbook v.I bismuth entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); A7 rhombohedral arsenic-group metal structure (shared with native arsenic + antimony)

native_sulfur._source: Anthony Handbook v.I sulfur entry; α-S8 ring molecular structure (Coppens et al. 1977 Acta Cryst. B33:1330-1335)

native_tellurium._source: Anthony Handbook v.I tellurium entry; helical chain structure (isotype with selenium; chiral)

dolomite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V dolomite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); ordered Ca-Mg layered calcite-derivative (R-3 vs calcite R-3c — symmetry broken by cation order)

siderite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V siderite entry; FeCO3, calcite-isostructural

rhodochrosite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V rhodochrosite entry; MnCO3, calcite-isostructural

smithsonite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V smithsonite entry; ZnCO3, calcite-isostructural

malachite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V malachite entry; Susse 1967 (Acta Cryst. 22:146-151) Cu2(CO3)(OH)2 refinement

azurite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V azurite entry; Cu3(CO3)2(OH)2 refinement (Zigan & Schuster 1972 Z. Krist. 135:416-436)

rosasite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V rosasite entry; (Cu,Zn)2(CO3)(OH)2, malachite-isostructural

aurichalcite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V aurichalcite entry; (Zn,Cu)5(CO3)2(OH)6 (Harding et al. 1994 Acta Cryst. C50:852-854)

wulfenite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V wulfenite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); PbMoO4 scheelite-type structure

scheelite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V scheelite entry; CaWO4, type for the scheelite structure family

powellite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V powellite entry; CaMoO4, scheelite-isostructural

stolzite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V stolzite entry; PbWO4 tetragonal polymorph (scheelite-class); dimorphic with raspite

raspite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V raspite entry; PbWO4 monoclinic polymorph; dimorphic with stolzite (Fujita et al. 1977 Acta Cryst. B33:162-168)

wolframite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V wolframite entry; (Fe,Mn)WO4 — solid-solution series between hübnerite (MnWO4) and ferberite (FeWO4)

ferrimolybdite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V ferrimolybdite entry; Fe2(MoO4)3·nH2O hydrated ferric molybdate (data sparse; values approximate; entry may need refinement when better refinement is available)

hematite._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.III hematite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); α-Fe2O3, corundum-isostructural (same R-3c hex setting)

cuprite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III cuprite entry; Cu2O, anti-fluorite-related cuprite structure (O at FCC sites, Cu in 1/4 of tetrahedral voids)

corundum._source: Anthony Handbook v.III corundum entry; α-Al2O3, type structure for the corundum group (hematite + ilmenite isostructural)

ruby._source: Cr-bearing corundum variety; same R-3c structure + lattice as corundum within composition error (Cr³⁺ substitution at Al sites; ~3-5% deviation max at high Cr)

sapphire._source: Fe-Ti-bearing corundum variety; same R-3c structure + lattice as corundum within composition error

chromite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III chromite entry; FeCr2O4 spinel-group, isostructural with MgAl2O4 spinel + Fe3O4 magnetite

pyrolusite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III pyrolusite entry; β-MnO2 rutile-isostructural (same P4_2/mnm group as rutile + cassiterite)

brucite._source: Anthony Handbook v.III brucite entry; Mg(OH)2 layered hydroxide, type structure for the brucite group (gibbsite, portlandite, etc.)

scorodite._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.IV scorodite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); FeAsO4·2H2O (variscite-group analogue)

mimetite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV mimetite entry; Pb5(AsO4)3Cl, apatite-group (chlorapatite arsenate analogue)

erythrite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV erythrite entry; Co3(AsO4)2·8H2O vivianite-group

annabergite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV annabergite entry; Ni3(AsO4)2·8H2O vivianite-group (erythrite-isostructural with Ni-for-Co)

olivenite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV olivenite entry; Cu2(AsO4)(OH); β ≈ 90° → pseudo-orthorhombic with latent pseudo-tet (a ≈ b) — same family as libethenite (Cu2(PO4)(OH))

conichalcite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV conichalcite entry; CaCu(AsO4)(OH) descloizite-group adelite analogue

pharmacolite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV pharmacolite entry; CaHAsO4·2H2O (gypsum-analogue with HAsO4²⁻ replacing SO4²⁻)

haidingerite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV haidingerite entry; CaHAsO4·H2O (pharmacolite-dehydrate)

austinite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV austinite entry; CaZn(AsO4)(OH) descloizite-group; pseudo-orthorhombic with cell similar to conichalcite

legrandite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV legrandite entry; Zn2(AsO4)(OH)·H2O (Mapimí + Sterling Hill localities)

koettigite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV koettigite entry; Zn3(AsO4)2·8H2O vivianite-group (erythrite-isostructural with Zn-for-Co)

pyromorphite._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.IV pyromorphite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); Pb5(PO4)3Cl apatite-group

vanadinite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV vanadinite entry; Pb5(VO4)3Cl apatite-group (chlorapatite vanadate analogue)

apatite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV apatite entry; Ca5(PO4)3(F,Cl,OH) — fluorapatite cell values; the entire apatite group shares this P6_3/m structure

clinobisvanite._source: BiVO4 monoclinic scheelite-distortion (Bierlein & Sleight 1975 Solid State Comm. 16:69-70); ferroelastic distortion from the tetragonal pucherite/scheelite-related parent — β ≈ 90° makes it pseudo-orthorhombic

descloizite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV descloizite entry; PbZn(VO4)(OH), type for the descloizite group

mottramite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV mottramite entry; PbCu(VO4)(OH), descloizite-isostructural with Cu-for-Zn

torbernite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV torbernite entry; Cu(UO2)2(PO4)2·12H2O autunite-group layered uranyl phosphate

zeunerite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV zeunerite entry; Cu(UO2)2(AsO4)2·12H2O torbernite-isostructural with As-for-P

carnotite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV carnotite entry; K2(UO2)2(VO4)2·3H2O layered uranyl vanadate

autunite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV autunite entry; Ca(UO2)2(PO4)2·11H2O — type species for the autunite group

uranospinite._source: Anthony Handbook v.IV uranospinite entry; Ca(UO2)2(AsO4)2·10H2O autunite-group with As-for-P

barite._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.V barite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); BaSO4 type structure for the barite group (celestine, anglesite isostructural)

celestine._source: Anthony Handbook v.V celestine entry; SrSO4 barite-group

anglesite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V anglesite entry; PbSO4 barite-group

jarosite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V jarosite entry; KFe3(SO4)2(OH)6 — type for the alunite supergroup; Stoffregen et al. 2000 (Reviews in Mineralogy v.40) crystal-chemistry review

alunite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V alunite entry; KAl3(SO4)2(OH)6 alunite-supergroup type species

brochantite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V brochantite entry; Cu4(SO4)(OH)6

antlerite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V antlerite entry; Cu3(SO4)(OH)4

anhydrite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V anhydrite entry; CaSO4 — chains of CaO8 polyhedra parallel to c, pseudo-tetragonal metrics (a ≈ c) but distinct b

selenite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V gypsum entry; same CaSO4·2H2O C2/c structure as gypsum (selenite = the transparent crystalline variety; not a separate mineral structurally)

chalcanthite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V chalcanthite entry; CuSO4·5H2O — triclinic; full cell α=82.27°, β=107.43°, γ=102.67° (Beevers & Lipson 1934 Proc. Roy. Soc. A146:570)

mirabilite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V mirabilite entry; Na2SO4·10H2O — Glauber's salt

thenardite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V thenardite entry; Na2SO4 — anhydrous polymorph of mirabilite

linarite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V linarite entry; PbCu(SO4)(OH)2 (Effenberger 1987 N. Jb. Mineral. Mh. 1987:127-135)

caledonite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V caledonite entry; Pb5Cu2(SO4)3(CO3)(OH)6 — mixed sulfate-carbonate

leadhillite._source: Anthony Handbook v.V leadhillite entry; Pb4(SO4)(CO3)2(OH)2 — β ≈ 90° makes it pseudo-orthorhombic; dimorphic with susannite (trigonal high-T)

apophyllite._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.II apophyllite entry; KCa4(Si8O20)F·8H2O fluorapophyllite — tetragonal sheet silicate

feldspar._source: Generic K-feldspar (orthoclase-equivalent); same C2/m structure + parameters as the orthoclase entry. The 'feldspar' species in vugg's data is treated as KAlSi3O8 monoclinic.

albite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II albite entry; NaAlSi3O8 — low-T ordered albite; full cell α=94.27°, β=116.57°, γ=87.66° (Harlow & Brown 1980 Amer. Mineral. 65:986-995)

topaz._source: Anthony Handbook v.II topaz entry; Al2(SiO4)(F,OH)2 (Ribbe & Gibbs 1971 Amer. Mineral. 56:24)

tourmaline._source: Anthony Handbook v.II tourmaline entry; representative schorl-dravite values; the tourmaline supergroup has the same R3m framework with varying chemistry

beryl._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.II beryl entry; Be3Al2(SiO3)6 hexagonal ring silicate

emerald._source: Cr-bearing beryl variety; same P6/mcc structure as beryl within composition error

aquamarine._source: Fe²⁺-bearing beryl variety; same P6/mcc structure as beryl

morganite._source: Mn-bearing beryl variety; same P6/mcc structure as beryl

heliodor._source: Fe³⁺-bearing beryl variety (yellow); same P6/mcc structure as beryl

spodumene._source: Anthony Handbook v.II spodumene entry; LiAlSi2O6 clinopyroxene Li-end-member

lepidolite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II lepidolite entry; K(Li,Al)3(Si,Al)4O10(F,OH)2 — Li-mica (1M polytype values; 2M1 and 3T polytypes also exist)

dioptase._source: Anthony Handbook v.II dioptase entry; CuSiO3·H2O 6-membered ring silicate

uranophane._source: Anthony Handbook v.II uranophane entry; Ca(UO2)2(SiO3OH)2·5H2O — uranyl silicate layered structure (alpha-uranophane polymorph values)

hemimorphite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II hemimorphite entry; Zn4Si2O7(OH)2·H2O — non-centrosymmetric (pyroelectric, piezoelectric)

willemite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II willemite entry; Zn2SiO4 phenakite-group

shattuckite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II shattuckite entry; Cu5(SiO3)4(OH)2 chain silicate (Pcab is non-standard Pbca setting)

datolite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II datolite entry; CaB(SiO4)(OH); β ≈ 90° → strongly pseudo-orthorhombic

vesuvianite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II vesuvianite entry; complex Ca-Al-Mg-Fe sorosilicate; large unit cell

grossular._source: Anthony Handbook v.II grossular entry; Ca3Al2(SiO4)3 garnet-group (typical garnet space group Ia-3d)

diopside._source: Anthony Handbook v.II diopside entry; CaMgSi2O6 clinopyroxene

pectolite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II pectolite entry; NaCa2Si3O8(OH); full cell α=90.5°, β=95.2°, γ=102.5° — pyroxenoid

wollastonite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II wollastonite entry; CaSiO3 1T polytype (the most common); full cell α=90.04°, β=95.4°, γ=103.4°

prehnite._source: Anthony Handbook v.II prehnite entry; Ca2Al(AlSi3O10)(OH)2 sheet silicate (Pncm pseudo-tetragonal a ≈ b)

sphalerite._source: Anthony Handbook of Mineralogy v.I sphalerite entry (handbookofmineralogy.org); ZnS — type structure for the zincblende family (F-43m, non-centrosymmetric cubic; the diamond cubic with Zn + S on the two FCC sublattices)

wurtzite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I wurtzite entry; ZnS hexagonal polymorph (high-T form vs sphalerite low-T) — type for the wurtzite structure family

chalcopyrite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I chalcopyrite entry; CuFeS2 sphalerite-superstructure with ordered Cu/Fe → tetragonal distortion (c/a = 1.97 ≈ 2, the doubled-cell signature)

arsenopyrite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I arsenopyrite entry; FeAsS — distorted marcasite-derivative (a ≈ b ≈ c, strongly pseudo-cubic monoclinic)

tetrahedrite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I tetrahedrite entry; (Cu,Ag,Fe,Zn,Hg)12Sb4S13 — tetrahedrite-group sulfosalt; non-centrosymmetric cubic

tennantite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I tennantite entry; (Cu,Fe)12As4S13 — As-end-member of the tetrahedrite-tennantite series (tetrahedrite-isostructural)

bornite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I bornite entry; Cu5FeS4 — HT cubic disordered form used here as the pseudo-cubic parent. LT form has a 4a-2a-2a superstructure (Pbca, a ≈ 10.94 × 21.86 × 21.86 Å); field samples are usually the metastable HT pseudo-cubic

chalcocite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I chalcocite entry; α-Cu2S (low-T monoclinic). Inverts to hexagonal P6_3/mmc above 103°C and to cubic Fm-3m above 435°C — multi-step polymorphism documented in Will et al. 2002

covellite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I covellite entry; CuS — layered structure with alternating Cu-S sheets; the long c-axis reflects the layer stacking

stibnite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I stibnite entry; Sb2S3 — type structure for the stibnite group; pseudo-tetragonal a ≈ c

bismuthinite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I bismuthinite entry; Bi2S3 — stibnite-isostructural with Bi-for-Sb

acanthite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I acanthite entry; α-Ag2S (LT monoclinic). Inverts to argentite (cubic Im-3m) above 173°C — paramorphic 'acanthite-after-argentite' habit is the typical field expression

argentite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I argentite entry; β-Ag2S (HT cubic), stable only above 173°C; field specimens are paramorphs (acanthite-after-argentite). Listed here for completeness with the vugg engine's argentite token

nickeline._source: Anthony Handbook v.I nickeline entry; NiAs — type structure for the nickeline group (NiAs-type, a fundamental binary sulfide/arsenide structure type)

millerite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I millerite entry; β-NiS (LT trigonal polymorph; dimorphic with hexagonal nickeline-isostructural γ-NiS above 379°C)

cobaltite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I cobaltite entry; CoAsS — pyrite-class (Pa-3) with ordered As-S replacing the pyrite S-S dumbbell

calaverite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I calaverite entry; AuTe2 — incommensurate modulated structure (the average is C2/m, β ≈ 90° → pseudo-orthorhombic; the modulation reflects Au-Te ordering)

proustite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I proustite entry; Ag3AsS3 — non-centrosymmetric trigonal; ruby silver

pyrargyrite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I pyrargyrite entry; Ag3SbS3 — proustite-isostructural with Sb-for-As; dark ruby silver

skutterudite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I skutterudite entry; CoAs3 — type structure for the skutterudite group; complex sphalerite-derivative with As4 squares filling the cubic framework

safflorite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I safflorite entry; CoAs2 loellingite-isostructural; member of the marcasite-loellingite structure family

rammelsbergite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I rammelsbergite entry; NiAs2 loellingite-isostructural

loellingite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I loellingite entry; FeAs2 — type structure for the loellingite group (same Pnnm space group as marcasite, with one M-X bond shortened)

enargite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I enargite entry; Cu3AsS4 — wurtzite superstructure with ordered Cu/As → orthorhombic distortion; the Sb-analogue is famatinite (tetragonal)

clausthalite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I clausthalite entry; PbSe — galena-isostructural with Se-for-S

greenockite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I greenockite entry; α-CdS — wurtzite-isostructural with Cd-for-Zn

hessite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I hessite entry; α-Ag2Te (LT monoclinic). Inverts to cubic Fm-3m form above 145°C

naumannite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I naumannite entry; β-Ag2Se (LT orthorhombic). Inverts to cubic Im-3m above 130°C — Ag chalcogenide series

sylvanite._source: Anthony Handbook v.I sylvanite entry; AuAgTe4 — ordered Au-Ag telluride; large β angle reflects the layered structure

cinnabar._source: Anthony Handbook v.I cinnabar entry; α-HgS — chiral trigonal (helical Hg-S chains along c); the famous cinnabar-twin is the {1010}/{0001} interpenetration twin

realgar._source: Anthony Handbook v.I realgar entry; α-As4S4 — molecular crystal with As4S4 cage molecules; photoactive (degrades to pararealgar β-As4S4 under light)

orpiment._source: Anthony Handbook v.I orpiment entry; As2S3 — layered structure of As2S3 sheets; β ≈ 90° → pseudo-orthorhombic
